<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>

// Note: push() and pop() are private, but we can observe their behavior
// through allocate() and deallocate() operations
template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    // Create a block_allocator with uint8_t and the specified element_count
    hexi::block_allocator<uint8_t, element_count> alloc;
    
    // Step 1: Allocate 3 blocks - this will call pop() 3 times internally
    uint8_t* block1 = alloc.allocate();
    uint8_t* block2 = alloc.allocate();
    uint8_t* block3 = alloc.allocate();
    
    // Step 2: Deallocate one block - this will call push() internally
    alloc.deallocate(block1);
    
    // Step 3: Allocate 2 more blocks - this will call pop() 2 more times
    uint8_t* block4 = alloc.allocate();
    uint8_t* block5 = alloc.allocate();
    
    // Step 4: Deallocate another block - another push() operation
    alloc.deallocate(block2);
    
    // The free list management is demonstrated through the sequence of:
    // - allocate() calls that remove blocks from the free list (via pop())
    // - deallocate() calls that return blocks to the free list (via push())
    // This shows how the linked list of free blocks is maintained internally.
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <algorithm> // for std::copy

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);

    stream << packet.packet_id;
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data);

    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Create a temporary buffer to hold the data from the span
    std::vector<uint8_t> temp_buffer(buffer.size());
    std::copy(buffer.begin(), buffer.end(), temp_buffer.begin());

    hexi::binary_stream stream(temp_buffer);
    CompactPacket packet;

    stream >> packet.packet_id;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data);

    return packet;
}
```

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    // Create a buffer with sufficient size to hold the input data
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    buffer.resize(input_data.size());
    
    // Create buffer_write_adaptor and write data
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> writer(buffer);
    
    // Check available space and write data
    if (writer.free() >= input_data.size()) {
        writer.write(input_data.data(), input_data.size());
    }
    
    // Create buffer_read_adaptor and read data back
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> reader(buffer);
    
    // Check available data size
    std::size_t data_size = reader.size();
    std::vector<uint8_t> output_data(data_size);
    
    // Read data back from buffer
    if (data_size > 0) {
        reader.read(output_data.data(), data_size);
    }
    
    return output_data;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    // Create buffer_read_adaptor
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> reader(buffer_data);
    
    // Peek data without advancing read pointer
    std::vector<uint8_t> peeked_data(peek_size);
    if (peek_size <= reader.size()) {
        reader.copy(peeked_data.data(), peek_size);
        
        // Consume the data by reading it (advances read pointer)
        reader.read(nullptr, peek_size); // Read and discard the data
    } else {
        // Handle case where peek_size exceeds available data
        peeked_data.resize(reader.size());
        if (peeked_data.size() > 0) {
            reader.copy(peeked_data.data(), peeked_data.size());
            reader.read(nullptr, peeked_data.size());
        }
    }
    
    return peeked_data;
}
```</answer>

   template<typename container_type, typename count_type>
   void read_container(container_type& container, const count_type count) {
       using cvalue_type = typename container_type::value_type;

       if constexpr(!memcpy_read<container_type, binary_stream>) {
           container.clear();

           const auto bytes = static_cast<size_type>(count * sizeof(cvalue_type));
           SAFE_READ(container.data(), bytes, void());
       } else {
           for(count_type i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
               cvalue_type value;
               *this >> value;
               container.emplace_back(std::move(value));

 Code for `deserialize_protocol_packet`:

   ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
       // Create a buffer_adaptor for the const span
       hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::span<const uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
       hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

       // Read the message_id
       uint32_t message_id;
       stream.read(&message_id);
       message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(message_id);

       // Read the count of data_values
       uint32_t count;
       stream.read(&count);
       count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count);

       // Read the vector
       std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
       stream.read_container(data_values, count);

       // Convert each element from big-endian to native
       for (auto& value : data_values) {
           value = hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
       }

       // Read the checksum
       uint32_t checksum;
       stream.read(&checksum);
       checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);

       return {message_id, std::move(data_values), checksum};
   }

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Create a buffer from the span
    hexi::Buffer buf(buffer);
    // Create a binary stream for reading
    hexi::binary_stream reader(buf);
    
    SensorInfo info;
    
    // Extract sensor name using view (null-terminated string)
    info.sensor_name = reader.view();
    
    // Check if there's enough data for sensor_id (4 bytes)
    reader.enforce_read_bounds(4);
    
    // Read sensor_id
    reader.read(&info.sensor_id);
    
    // Skip the remaining data (temperatures and pressure)
    // The total size of the buffer is buffer.size(), and we've read:
    //   - sensor_name: variable length (including null terminator)
    //   - sensor_id: 4 bytes
    // We need to skip the rest.
    // We can calculate the remaining size by: buffer.size() - reader.total_read()
    // But note: the reader might have advanced by the length of the sensor_name and 4 bytes.
    // However, the problem says to skip the remaining data.
    reader.skip(buffer.size() - reader.total_read());
    
    return info;
}
```

 However, note that the `hexi::Buffer` type is not defined in the provided reference materials. We must assume that it is a type that can be constructed from a `std::span` and that it meets the requirements of the `hexi::binary_stream`.

 Also, note that the `hexi::binary_stream` constructor takes a `buf_type&` and an optional `read_limit`. We are using the default `read_limit` (0) for both reading and writing.

 The above code is based on the provided specifications and the `hexi` library code snippets.
